title: cronsplain schedule golden vectors: candidates/vectors.js (candidate-agnostic interface + ground truth)
description: Candidate-agnostic golden-vector file for the schedule tournament (mirrors mdtoc's vectors.py role). Authored BEFORE any candidate. Fixes the nextOccurrences interface + the hand-computed ground truth so candidates (T-047/T-048) and the verdict (T-049) can self-check. Depends on T-045: it require()s lib/parser.js to build parsed inputs. Imports NO candidate.
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/candidates/vectors.js. Touch no other file. require()s ../lib/parser (parser.parse) to build parsed inputs. Imports NO schedule_*.js candidate.

INTERFACE (documented in the module docstring; every candidate implements it): nextOccurrences(parsed, fromDate, count) -> Date[]. Returns the next `count` UTC Date objects whose minute matches `parsed` (the parse-result from parser.parse), each strictly AFTER fromDate (EXCLUSIVE), minute-aligned (seconds=0, ms=0), ascending. Applies DOM/DOW OR coupling: when parsed.domRestricted && parsed.dowRestricted, a day matches if day-of-month OR day-of-week matches; if only one restricted, only that field constrains; if neither, all days.

VECTORS: array of ground-truth cases { expr, from (ISO-Z string), count, expected (array of ISO-Z strings) }. MUST cover: (a) simple step '*/15 * * * *'; (b) daily '0 0 * * *'; (c) month rollover (a late-in-month match rolling to next month); (d) YEAR rollover (Dec 31 -> Jan 1); (e) leap Feb 29 '0 0 29 2 *' -> only leap years; (f) DOM/DOW OR quirk '0 0 13 * 5' (13th OR Friday) and '0 0 1 * 1' (1st OR Monday); (g) one-field-* cases (dom restricted with dow=*, and dow restricted with dom=*); (h) EXCLUSIVITY: a `from` landing exactly on a matching minute must NOT return that minute (returns the next). Each expected value hand-computed and annotated with a // comment citing the rule it exercises (OR-coupling / exclusivity / rollover / leap).

runAgainst(nextOccurrencesFn) -> { passed:int, total:int, failures:[{expr, expected, got}] }: for each VECTOR, parse expr via parser.parse, call nextOccurrencesFn(parsed, new Date(from), count), map returned Dates to ISO-Z (d.toISOString(), which is already Z; ensure seconds=0), compare to expected; accumulate. Imports NO candidate (the fn is passed in). module.exports = { VECTORS, runAgainst, INTERFACE_DOC }.
Provide at least ~10 VECTORS total spanning the categories above. The `expected` arrays are the TRUSTED GROUND TRUTH — compute them carefully by hand and cite each.
SHARED CONVENTIONS: see T-044 acceptance item. CommonJS, zero deps, UTC everywhere.
HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim/lock(candidates/vectors.js)/in_progress/handoff --to-role verifier; NEVER self-mark done; --agent <you>.

VERIFY (in handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ `node -e "const v=require('./candidates/vectors'); console.log(typeof v.runAgainst, Array.isArray(v.VECTORS), v.VECTORS.length)"` prints "function true <N>" with N>=10.
